title: andropogon nardus subsp. flexuosus
intro: Andropogon nardus subsp. flexuosus, commonly known as Australian sand sedge, is a subspecies of the Andropogon nardus plant that is native to Australia. This plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Andropogon nardus subsp. flexuosus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
